# Cooktown Mayors in the Eighties

**Source:** The Townsville Daily Bulletin (Qld.: 1907–1909; 1912–1966)
**Date:** Wednesday, 6 October 1954, Page 6
**Byline:** Special Representative
**Dateline:** Cooktown, October 5

Cooktown had two mayors on Friday, from Townsville and Bowen, the day on which the conference of the North Queensland Local Authorities' Association was held.

But in its earlier days, and for about a half century, Cooktown was a municipality, with its mayor and town council.

Direct evidence of this dating back to 1885, is to be found on a monument in the main street.

The monument had been erected to perpetuate the heroism of Mrs. Watson, who made history as the result of her unremitting care to her child when on Lizard Island.

The names of the two mayors are on the obelisk, one being Alderman Edward D'Arcy, the date being 1885.

On another side of the pillar is the name of Alderman John Davis, the date being 1885.
